messages.reasoning_content:
The issue is with the `checkWinner` function returning `[3]int` but the caller expects `[]int`. I need to convert the slice. Let me fix this.

Also, I notice the `playerX` and `playerO` functions I created earlier aren't being used - the game flow is inline. The user asked for `playerX` and `playerO` functions. Let me refactor to use those functions properly.

Let me also fix:
1. The type mismatch in `checkWinner` return
2. Actually use `playerX()` and `playerO()` functions as requested
3. Remove unused `getValidMoves` function
4. Clean up the code

Let me rewrite this more cleanly.

22: func renderBoard(b [9]string, sc scores, highlight []int, blink bool) {
23: 	var sb strings.Builder
24: 
25: 	sb.WriteString("\n")
26: 	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("\033[37mScore: X: %d | O: %d | Draws: %d\033[0m\n\n", sc.X, sc.O, sc.Draws))
27: 
28: 	for i := 0; i < 9; i++ {
29: 		if i == 3 || i == 6 {
30: 			sb.WriteString("----------\n")
31: 		}
32: 		if i%3 == 0 && i > 0 {
33: 			sb.WriteString("\n")
34: 		}
35: 
36: 		val := b[i]
37: 		isHighlight := false
38: 		for _, h := range highlight {
39: 			if h == i {
40: 				isHighlight = true
41: 				break
42: 			}
43: 		}
44: 
45: 		switch val {
46: 		case "X":
47: 			if isHighlight && blink {
48: 				sb.WriteString("\033[5m\033[31;1mX\033[0m")
49: 			} else {
50: 				sb.WriteString("\033[31;1mX\033[0m")
51: 			}
52: 		case "O":
53: 			if isHighlight && blink {
54: 				sb.WriteString("\033[5m\033[32;1mO\033[0m")
55: 			} else {
56: 				sb.WriteString("\033[32;1mO\033[0m")
57: 			}
58: 		default:
59: 			sb.WriteString("\033[37m" + val + "\033[0m")
60: 		}
61: 
62: 		if i%3 != 2 {
63: 			sb.WriteString(" | ")
64: 		}
65: 	}
66: 
67: 	sb.WriteString("\n\n")
68: 	fmt.Print(sb.String())
69: }

74: 		reader := bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin)

86: 			// already validated above

110: func getValidMoves(b [9]string) []int {
111: 	var moves []int
112: 	for i, v := range b {
113: 		if v == "X" || v == "O" {
114: 			continue
115: 		}
116: 		moves = append(moves, i)
117: 	}
118: 	return moves

121: func checkWinner(b [9]string) (int, []int) {
122: 	lines := [][3]int{
123: 		{0, 1, 2}, {3, 4, 5}, {6, 7, 8}, // rows
124: 		{0, 3, 6}, {1, 4, 7}, {2, 5, 8}, // cols
125: 		{0, 4, 8}, {2, 4, 6}, // diags
126: 	}
127: 
128: 	for _, line := range lines {
129: 		if b[line[0]] != "" && b[line[0]] == b[line[1]] && b[line[1]] == b[line[2]] {
130: 			player := 0
131: 			if b[line[0]] == "X" {
132: 				player = 1
133: 			} else {
134: 				player = 2
135: 			}
136: 			return player, line
137: 		}
138: 	}
139: 	return 0, nil
140: }
141: 
142: func checkDraw(b [9]string) bool {
143: 	for _, v := range b {
144: 		if v == "X" || v == "O" {
145: 			continue
146: 		}
147: 		return false
148: 	}
149: 	return true
150: }

152: func playGame(sc *scores) {
153: 	var board [9]string
154: 	for i := 0; i < 9; i++ {
155: 		board[i] = strconv.Itoa(i + 1)
156: 	}
157: 
158: 	clearScreen()
159: 	renderBoard(board, *sc, nil, false)
160: 
161: 	for {
162: 		// Player X's turn
163: 		for {
164: 			fmt.Print("\033[31;1mPlayer X's turn. Enter a number (1-9): \033[0m")
165: 			reader := bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin)
166: 			input, _ := reader.ReadString('\n')
167: 			input = strings.TrimSpace(input)
168: 
169: 			n, err := strconv.Atoi(input)
170: 			if err != nil || n < 1 || n > 9 {
171: 				fmt.Println("Invalid input. Enter a number between 1 and 9.")
172: 				continue
173: 			}
174: 
175: 			idx := n - 1
176: 			if board[idx] != "X" && board[idx] != "O" {
177: 				board[idx] = "X"
178: 				break
179: 			}
180: 			fmt.Println("Space already taken. Choose another.")
181: 		}
182: 
183: 		clearScreen()
184: 		renderBoard(board, *sc, nil, false)
185: 
186: 		winner, winCells := checkWinner(board)
187: 		if winner == 1 {
188: 			sc.X++
189: 			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
190: 			clearScreen()
191: 			renderBoard(board, *sc, winCells, true)
192: 			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
193: 			fmt.Printf("\n\033[31;1mGame Over! X wins!\033[0m\n\n")
194: 			return
195: 		}
196: 
197: 		if checkDraw(board) {
198: 			sc.Draws++
199: 			clearScreen()
200: 			renderBoard(board, *sc, nil, false)
201: 			fmt.Printf("\n\033[37mGame Over! It's a draw!\033[0m\n\n")
202: 			return
203: 		}
204: 
205: 		// Player O's turn
206: 		for {
207: 			fmt.Print("\033[32;1mPlayer O's turn. Enter a number (1-9): \033[0m")
208: 			reader := bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin)
209: 			input, _ := reader.ReadString('\n')
210: 			input = strings.TrimSpace(input)
211: 
212: 			n, err := strconv.Atoi(input)
213: 			if err != nil || n < 1 || n > 9 {
214: 				fmt.Println("Invalid input. Enter a number between 1 and 9.")
215: 				continue
216: 			}
217: 
218: 			idx := n - 1
219: 			if board[idx] != "X" && board[idx] != "O" {
220: 				board[idx] = "O"
221: 				break
222: 			}
223: 			fmt.Println("Space already taken. Choose another.")
224: 		}
225: 
226: 		clearScreen()
227: 		renderBoard(board, *sc, nil, false)
228: 
229: 		winner, winCells = checkWinner(board)
230: 		if winner == 2 {
231: 			sc.O++
232: 			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
233: 			clearScreen()
234: 			renderBoard(board, *sc, winCells, true)
235: 			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
236: 			fmt.Printf("\n\033[32;1mGame Over! O wins!\033[0m\n\n")
237: 			return
238: 		}
239: 
240: 		if checkDraw(board) {
241: 			sc.Draws++
242: 			clearScreen()
243: 			renderBoard(board, *sc, nil, false)
244: 			fmt.Printf("\n\033[37mGame Over! It's a draw!\033[0m\n\n")
245: 			return
246: 		}
247: 	}
248: }
249: 
250: func main() {
251: 	var sc scores
252: 
253: 	for {
254: 		playGame(&sc)
255: 
256: 		fmt.Print("Play again? (y/n): ")
257: 		reader := bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin)
258: 		input, _ := reader.ReadString('\n')
259: 		input = strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(input))
260: 
261: 		if input != "y" {
262: 			fmt.Println("Thanks for playing!")
263: 			break
264: 		}
265: 
266: 		clearScreen()
267: 	}
268: }
